Why are you interested in this role?: What pulls me to this role is that I genuinely love the front end of the people function—finding the right person and giving them a candidate experience that makes them want to say yes. At Kronos Research I owned a tough international search end-to-end, drafting the JD, sourcing passive talent, and personalizing outreach until applications grew 566% in two months. The part I enjoyed most was the craft of it: the organization, the follow-through, the relationship-building. A Business Recruiter role is that instinct made into a job, and I'd get to do it at the pace and scale of a company that's actually growing.
Why do you want to work at Brex?: Two reasons, honestly. First, the pace—I do my best work when there are multiple searches moving at once and I have to stay organized and decisive, and a high-growth fintech is exactly that environment. Second, this is work I do well: I treat recruiting as a system to diagnose and improve, not just reqs to close. With a master's in Organizational Psychology, I read the talent market through data, partner closely with hiring managers on strategy, and track pipeline health so I know what's actually working. I'd bring that same care to building talent pipelines that scale with Brex.
How do you approach partnering with hiri: I over-invest in alignment early—I'd rather spend the extra time on an intake conversation than risk sourcing against the wrong picture. My approach is to diagnose before designing: I ask what success in the role really looks like, where past hires have struggled, and what the market realistically offers, then build the search strategy around that shared understanding. I keep stakeholders close with regular check-ins on pipeline health, so course-correcting is cheap and fast. That habit comes from my consulting and HRBP work, where adoption always depended on getting the stakeholders bought in first.
Tell us about yourself / anything else: I'm an analytical organizer with a growth mindset, grounded in an MS in Applied Organizational Psychology from USC. My supervisor once called me the most organized person he's ever worked with—that organization is what lets me run multiple full-cycle searches without dropping a candidate. I have extensive hands-on experience in Greenhouse and pick up new ATS and sourcing tools quickly. I'd love to bring that combination of structure and genuine people-focus to your Talent team.
